root_cause:
Evidence is complete and consistent. Let me summarize the causal chain:

**What it is NOT (look-alikes ruled out):**
- **Datastore failure:** memcached p99 1.7ms, Postgres p99 4.2ms, both low CPU/conns — server-side processing is fast in every trace. Not dependency_failure.
- **Connection-pool exhaustion:** pgx pool 7 acquired of 18, explicitly "pool not exhausted." Postgres 41/200 conns. Not resource_exhaustion of the pool.
- **Bad deploy:** rollout is 6 days old, clean, image pulled 6d ago. Not bad_deploy.
- **Node resource exhaustion:** node CPU 41%, no MemoryPressure/OOM/eviction. The aggregate node view *hides* the real problem.

**What it IS:** Every failure is node-local to **pool-a-3f21**. On that node the GKE Dataplane V2 **Cilium agent (anetd)** is pegged at 2.64 vCPU (baseline 0.2), driven 1:1 by a TCP connection open/close rate of 2100/s and conntrack GC churn of 4.1M events/min from a co-located `egress-worker` endpoint. The saturated eBPF datapath drops ~850 pps in-cluster on pool-a only — manifesting as dropped SYN/connect packets and read i/o timeouts to *both* memcached and Postgres (and flapping readiness probes). The identical code path on pool-b (anetd 0.19 vCPU, 0 drops) succeeds in 22ms.

ROOT_CAUSE: The GKE Dataplane V2 Cilium agent (anetd) on node gke-incidentio-prod-pool-a-3f21 is CPU-saturated (2.64 vCPU vs ~0.2 baseline) from extreme TCP connection churn (2100 conns/s, 4.1M conntrack GC events/min) generated by a co-located high-churn `egress-worker` pod. The overloaded eBPF datapath drops ~850 pps in-cluster, causing dropped SYN/read packets that surface in the api pod as intermittent memcached read i/o timeouts and Postgres connection-acquire deadlines — the datastores and the connection pool are healthy.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: saturation
FIX: Stop the connection-churn source on pool-a — patch the `egress-worker` to reuse connections (enable keep-alive/connection pooling) and, as immediate relief, cordon+drain/reschedule the egress-worker off the api node so the saturated anetd cilium-agent recovers; longer term raise anetd CPU limits and tune Cilium conntrack GC.
trap_actions: Disable GKE Dataplane V2 (rebuild the cluster off Dataplane V2) — a cluster-wide rebuild to fix a single workload's connection-churn problem; does not address the churn and risks broad disruption.

Endless DB/cache connection-pool tuning — doubling max DB connection lifetime + static pool, raising memcached MaxIdleConns 2->20 — only briefly helps and regresses; never addresses the dropped packets.
Migrate to self-hosted in-cluster memcached blaming the GKE-managed cache — had no effect.
Credit the unrelated runaway-external-API fix and close the incident — it only correlated in time; timeouts recur.
